user_query: Help me set up and run February 2026 usage-based billing for two customers from the provided structured inputs. Read these files under input/: input/customers.csv, input/meters.json, input/rate_plans.json, input/readings.csv, input/usage_events.csv, input/prepaid_credits.csv, and input/adjustments.csv. Use them to: (1) assign the defined rate plans to the listed meters, (2) create billing periods for 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-29 per meter and customer, (3) compute consumption for electricity from start/end readings and for SaaS by summing usage events within February (inclusive), (4) rate the consumption using the provided tiered pricing rules, applying base charges and any minimum charges, (5) apply adjustments, (6) calculate tax at 10% on the subtotal after adjustments (but before prepaid credits), (7) apply prepaid credits at the invoice level after tax (cannot reduce below $0), and (8) produce invoice drafts. Tier math: tiers are [start, end) with the last tier open-ended; allocate usage sequentially across tiers; currency rounding to 2 decimals. Minimum charge checks apply to base_charge + usage_charge before adjustments. Write these deliverables: (a) output/billing_periods.json (array of period objects with id, customer_id, meter_id, period_start, period_end, total_consumption, base_charge, usage_charge, adjustments_total, subtotal, tax_amount, grand_total), (b) output/invoices.json (array with invoice_id, customer_id, billing_period_ids, subtotal, tax_amount, prepaid_applied, grand_total), and (c) output/summary.md (clear narrative summary of what you did, per-customer totals, and any assumptions). Please include a brief note in the summary on any assumptions you made and confirm before you would run a bill cycle or generate invoices in a real system. Use USD for all amounts.
